project.name: trustanchor
project.currentFocus: Forensic light-table UI redesign and API smoke validation
durableFacts: Docker development image installs Node dependencies with npm ci during build, not at container startup.
The web service host port is configurable through WEB_PORT with default 3000.
Bootstrap admin authentication is environment-backed through BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME and BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD.
The active UI concept is a forensic document light table with layered evidence sheets, registration rails, and CSS-only inspection-light motion.

lastAchievements: Removed runtime npm install from docker-compose web startup.
Added Docker build cache usage for npm ci and a named .next cache volume for development.
Created a local bootstrap admin test account in .env without committing secret values.
Fixed development proof verification by sharing fallback signing keys through globalThis across Next.js dev route bundles.
Redesigned landing, login, not-found, and verification result UI around the forensic light-table concept.
pendingIssues: Next.js 14.2.5 and npm audit reported security advisories during Docker build.
Playwright is not installed locally, so no browser screenshot regression was run.
nextBestActions: Upgrade Next.js to a patched compatible version in a separate dependency update.
Add a focused integration test for certificate issuance followed by public verification.
Run a visual browser pass on desktop and mobile after installing an approved screenshot tool.
validationState: WEB_PORT=3001 docker compose up -d web passed and /api/health returned ok.
API smoke test passed for health, OpenAPI, login, certificate template creation, certificate issuance, and public verification on port 3000.
Route smoke test passed for /, /login, /api/health, /api/openapi.json, and /verify/TA-E6DA8273A2.
npm run typecheck and npm run test passed; npm run lint passed inside the web container.
